sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the full picture:
- B (black): top bar (cols 0-27), main structure (rows 9-58, cols 9-53), bottom bar (cols 36-63)
- O (orange): right background (rows 1-62, cols 32-63)
- S (sky blue): two blue squares (rows 54-58, cols 24-28 and 34-38)
- W (white): top bar (cols 28-63), bottom bar (cols 0-35)
- Y (yellow): left background (rows 1-62, cols 0-31)
So the background is split: yellow on left (cols 0-31), orange on right (cols 32-63).
The black structure is in the middle (rows 9-58, cols 9-53).
The blues are at the bottom of the structure.
Current bars: B=28, W=36 (top), W=36, B=28 (bottom)
Let me think about what the goal could be. The bars seem to be shifting. Maybe the goal is to reach a specific bar configuration.
Let me look at the initial state. The initial bars were B=45, W=19 (top), W=19, B=45 (bottom).
The background split is at col 32 (yellow 0-31, orange 32-63). The total width is 64.
Maybe the goal is to make the bars match the background split? That would be B=32, W=32 (top), W=32, B=32 (bottom).
But I've been clicking and the bars are now at B=28, W=36. I need to shift them back by 4 pixels.
Wait, but I don't know how to shift them in the opposite direction. Let me try clicking on the right blue instead of the left blue.
Actually, I already tried that and it shifted in the same direction. Let me try clicking on the right side of the right blue.
Or maybe I need to move the blues to a different position and then click. Let me try moving the blues up first.
Actually, let me try a completely different approach. Let me try clicking on the yellow or orange areas inside the black structure.

</tool_call>
